name: gemini
description: Generate text, images, embeddings, and token counts and manage uploaded files with Google Gemini models via the Gemini API

envVars.name: GEMINI_API_KEY
envVars.description: Gemini API key from Google AI Studio (starts with AIza)

tools.name: List Models
tools.description: List the Gemini models available to the API key

pageSize.description: Maximum models to return per page (max 1000)

pageToken.description: Page token from a previous list call

historicalSummary.omitted: model capability details and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Get Model
tools.description: Get details about a specific Gemini model

model.description: Model ID without the models/ prefix, e.g. gemini-2.0-flash

tools.name: Generate Content
tools.description: Generate a model response from text or multimodal content

model.description: Model ID without the models/ prefix, e.g. gemini-2.0-flash

systemInstruction.description: System instructions, e.g. {"parts":[{"text":"You are a helpful assistant"}]}

tools.name: Embed Content
tools.description: Create an embedding vector for input text

model.description: Embedding model ID without the models/ prefix, e.g. gemini-embedding-001

content.description: Content to embed, e.g. {"parts":[{"text":"Text to embed"}]}

taskType.description: Embedding task type, e.g. RETRIEVAL_QUERY, RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT, CLUSTERING

outputDimensionality.description: Reduced dimension for the output embedding (supported models only)

tools.name: Count Tokens
tools.description: Count the tokens a prompt would use for a given model

model.description: Model ID without the models/ prefix, e.g. gemini-2.0-flash

contents.description: Contents to count tokens for, e.g. [{"role":"user","parts":[{"text":"Hello"}]}]

tools.name: Generate Image
tools.description: Generate or edit an image with a Nano Banana model via generateContent; the response returns image parts as base64 inlineData

model.description: Image model ID without the models/ prefix: gemini-3.1-flash-image (Nano Banana 2, fast), gemini-3-pro-image (Nano Banana Pro, highest quality), or gemini-2.5-flash-image (Nano Banana, legacy)

contents.description: Prompt contents, e.g. [{"role":"user","parts":[{"text":"A watercolor fox in a forest"}]}]; include reference images as inlineData parts for editing

generationConfig.description: Must set responseModalities to receive images, e.g. {"responseModalities":["TEXT","IMAGE"],"imageConfig":{"aspectRatio":"1:1"}}

tools.name: Edit Image
tools.description: Edit an existing image with a Nano Banana model: send the source image as a base64 inlineData part plus a text instruction (add/remove/modify elements, change style, adjust color grading); the edited image is returned as base64 inlineData

model.description: Image model ID without the models/ prefix: gemini-3.1-flash-image (Nano Banana 2, fast), gemini-3-pro-image (Nano Banana Pro, highest quality), or gemini-2.5-flash-image (Nano Banana, legacy)

contents.description: One user turn with the source image and the edit instruction, e.g. [{"role":"user","parts":[{"inlineData":{"mimeType":"image/png","data":"<base64>"}},{"text":"Remove the background and make it a sunset"}]}]; add more turns to iterate on the same image

generationConfig.description: Must set responseModalities to receive images, e.g. {"responseModalities":["TEXT","IMAGE"]}

tools.name: Generate Image (Imagen)
tools.description: Generate text-to-image samples with an Imagen 4 model via the predict endpoint; returns base64-encoded images in predictions

model.description: Imagen model ID without the models/ prefix: imagen-4.0-generate-001 (standard), imagen-4.0-ultra-generate-001 (ultra), or imagen-4.0-fast-generate-001 (fast)

instances.description: Prompt instances, e.g. [{"prompt":"A photorealistic lighthouse at dawn"}]

tools.name: Upload File
tools.description: Upload content to the Files API for use in later generateContent calls via its file URI; this simple media upload sends the decoded bytes directly as the request body (a custom displayName requires the multipart or resumable X-Goog-Upload protocol), and files expire after 48 hours

uploadType.description: Upload protocol; 'media' sends the request body directly as the file content

contentType.description: MIME type of the uploaded content

content.description: Base64-encoded file content; the decoded bytes are sent as the request body

tools.name: List Files
tools.description: List files uploaded to the Files API for this project

pageSize.description: Maximum files to return per page (max 100)

pageToken.description: Page token from a previous list call

historicalSummary.omitted: file hashes, timestamps, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Get File
tools.description: Get metadata for an uploaded file, including its uri and processing state

fileId.description: File ID without the files/ prefix, e.g. abc-123

tools.name: Delete File
tools.description: Delete an uploaded file before its automatic 48-hour expiry

fileId.description: File ID without the files/ prefix, e.g. abc-123

prompts.title: List Gemini models
prompts.prompt: List the Gemini models available to my API key and summarize their token limits.

prompts.title: Generate a response
prompts.prompt: Use Gemini to generate a response to a prompt I provide.

prompts.title: Generate an image
prompts.prompt: Generate an image with a Nano Banana model from a description I provide.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Google Gemini API Setup

steps.title: Sign in to Google AI Studio
steps.description: Go to https://aistudio.google.com and sign in with a Google account. The free tier works for testing without billing.

steps.title: Create an API key
steps.description: Open https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey and click 'Create API key'. Choose an existing Google Cloud project or let AI Studio create one for you.

steps.title: Store the key
steps.description: Copy the key and add it to your .env file as GEMINI_API_KEY=AIza...

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run the List Models tool to confirm the key works. A 400 with API_KEY_INVALID means the key is wrong or restricted.

notes: This connector uses the Gemini Developer API key (x-goog-api-key header), not Google OAuth — do not use Google Cloud OAuth credentials here
Free-tier keys are rate limited; attach a billing-enabled Google Cloud project for higher limits
Generation and embedding calls are billed per token on paid tiers
